text:
There once was a boy named Freddy. Freddy got lost one day because he didn't know how to read. He wandered the country roads, getting lost at each corner.

As dusk approached, Freddy noticed a soft luminescence in the distance. Curious, he pressed on toward it and arrived at a cozy little clearing. There, sitting by a crackling fire, was a kindly old owl. The owl eyed Freddy warily, but soon flicked its beak open in a welcoming squawk. "Where did you come from?" the owl asked with a warm voice.

Freddy took a deep breath and decided to tell a tall tale. "Oh, I've come from far across the plains! I've seen buffalo herds and wild prairies. I've traveled paths worn by generations of settlers."

The owl laughed heartily. "Well, young traveler, you've got quite the adventure in you yet. Come closer and tell me your story."

Freddy huffed
